purpose: settings.output_to_file=true on the exact output-to-file-default program: reason() rebinds the process's stdout to ./pyreason_output_<wall-clock>.txt opened for append (pyreason.py:1513-1514, re-opened onto the same name at :1541-1542) and never restores or flushes it. The output_file probe flushes and reads the file back from the capture's confined directory: name canonicalized to pyreason_output_<timestamp>.txt (the stamp at pyreason.py:1511 is run schedule, not engine behavior — recorded canonicalization), contents exact — the verbose reason-time prints (Filtering/Optimizing lines, Timestep 0-2, the convergence line, Fixed Point iterations) that the default twin left on process stdout. The nondefault-true-redirect class (setting:output_to_file) plus the default name 'pyreason_output' interpolated into the redirect path; reasoning probe digests matched output-to-file-default's at authoring (the redirect moves prints, never reasoning).
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:output_to_file nondefault-true-redirect; hello-world program with the knob set true and the redirect file compared
